purpose: User-perspective journey contract for the whole Kolm product: every major product surface must expose account, CLI, TUI, API, customization, and proof paths.

research_references.why_it_matters: Modern distillation products combine captured completions, evals, and fine-tuning; Kolm must add provider-independent artifacts and local/runtime proof.
Claude is a native provider surface, not an afterthought behind OpenAI-compatible routing.
Gateway table stakes now include analytics, logging, caching, rate limiting, retries, and fallback.
Unified APIs, budgets, usage monitoring, load balancing, and fallbacks are baseline UX for AI gateways.
Production AI observability should use common GenAI spans, events, metrics, and provider attributes where possible.
Self-hosted serving engines increasingly expose OpenAI-compatible APIs; Kolm should route to them and compile away repeated work.
Portable inference has to target established runtimes and execution providers, not a proprietary-only runtime.
Edge/mobile deployment needs device-aware export targets for constrained hardware.
Enterprise buyers expect SSO, SCIM, audit logs, admin workflows, authorization, and secure vault patterns.

global_ux_requirements: Every journey has a visible account entrypoint, a CLI equivalent, a TUI view or command, and an API route.
Every journey exposes customization across provider/model, compute, storage, privacy, runtime, deployment, governance, or proof dimensions.
Every cloud or enterprise journey must show missing configuration without printing secret values.
Every training or compile journey must show holdout/eval proof and failure diagnostics before promotion.
Every interactive surface must be keyboard/touch usable and cannot depend on hover-only menus.

journeys.user_story: As a developer, I can replace my OpenAI, Claude, OpenRouter, Gemini-compatible, or self-hosted base URL and start capturing only the calls I choose.
As a regulated operator, I can inspect what was captured, redact sensitive values, export rows, and switch to zero-retention or differential-privacy aggregates.
As a model builder, I can convert repeated work into labeled train/eval sets with review queues, simulations, and holdout splits.
As a user without perfect local hardware, I can train or distill via local compute, remote SSH GPU, managed GPU, Together, OpenAI/Claude teachers, or BYOC.
As a builder, I can choose real backbones such as Gemma 3, Gemma 3n, MedGemma, EmbeddingGemma, Qwen, Phi, Llama, Mistral, and SmolLM by task, license, modality, memory, and target device.
happy_path: bind to compile or distill spec

journeys.user_story: As a multimodal user, I can tokenize images, audio, video, PDFs, text, and code into searchable Markdown sidecars with local feature tokens and optional captions or transcripts.
As an operator, I can compile a task into a signed .kolm artifact, verify it offline, diff versions, and export runtime-specific assets.
As an app developer, I can run a compiled artifact locally, in the browser, on edge/server runtimes, or behind OpenAI-compatible fallback chains.
As a team with no GPU, I can configure Cloudflare R2, S3-compatible storage, AWS, Supabase, Modal, RunPod, Lambda, Together, or remote SSH and still build artifacts.
As an edge or enterprise user, I can detect devices, choose compatible runtimes, create team reverse tunnels, test installability, and push artifacts to local, SSH, HTTP, or air-gapped targets.
As an enterprise admin, I can control tenants, scoped keys, approvals, audit logs, billing, privacy, compliance export, and SSO/SCIM-ready identity.
As an agent-platform user, I can compile artifacts as MCP tools, install them into Claude/Cursor/Continue/Cline, publish or pull registry artifacts, and replay hashed run logs.
